metadata.title: 神はわがやぐら
metadata.subtitle: Ein feste Burg ist unser Gott (Luther) — Japanese (讃美歌, 1903)
metadata.lyricist: Martin Luther (1483–1546), "Ein feste Burg ist unser Gott" (1527–1529) — Japanese translation by the editorial committee of 讃美歌 (1903)
metadata.x_pd_basis: Luther's original German lyrics and tune (1527–1529) are PD by > 470 years. Japanese translation appeared in 讃美歌 (1903) — Meiji 36, > 120 years ago, edited by a committee whose members all died before 1955. Japanese Wikipedia quotes the 1903 text in full

chordpro:
{title: 神はわがやぐら}
{subtitle: Ein feste Burg (Luther, 1529) — Japanese, 讃美歌 (1903) — "the battle hymn of the Reformation"}
{key: C}
{time: 4/4}
{tempo: 80}
{comment: Bach used this melody in Cantata BWV 80; Mendelssohn quoted it in his Reformation Symphony.}

{start_of_verse: 1}
[C]神はわが[G]やぐら [C]わが強き[G]盾
kami wa waga yagura, waga tsuyoki tate
[Am]苦しめる[F]ときの [C]近き[G]助[C]けぞ
kurushimeru toki no, chikaki tasuke zo
[C]おのが[F]力 [C]おのが[G]知恵を
onoga chikara, onoga chie wo
[Am]たのみと[Em]せる [F]よみの[G]おさぞ
tanomi to seru, yomi no osa zo
[C]げにお[F]ぞま[C]し[G]き[C]
geni ozomashiki
{end_of_verse}

{start_of_verse: 2}
[C]いかに強[G]くとも [C]いかでか[G]頼まん
ikani tsuyoku tomo, ikade ka tanoman
[Am]やがてはく[F]つべき [C]人のち[G]から[C]を
yagate wa kutsubeki, hito no chikara wo
[C]われと[F]ともに [C]たたかい[G]たもう
ware to tomo ni, tatakai tamou
[Am]イエス[Em]きみこそ [F]万軍(ばんぐん)[G]の主なる
iesu kimi koso, bangun no shu naru
[C]あまつ[F]おお[C]神[G]ぞ[C]
amatsu oo kami zo
{end_of_verse}

{start_of_verse: 3}
[C]悪鬼(おに)は世[G]にみちて [C]よしおど[G]すとも
oni wa yo ni michite, yoshi odosu tomo
[Am]神のまこ[F]とこそ [C]わがうち[G]にあ[C]れ
kami no makoto koso, waga uchi ni are
[C]よみの[F]おさよ [C]ほえたけ[G]りて
yomi no osa yo, hoe takerite
[Am]せまりく[Em]とも [F]主のさ[G]ばきは
semari ku tomo, shu no sabaki wa
[C]なが上[F]にあ[C]り[G][C]
naga ue ni ari
{end_of_verse}

{start_of_verse: 4}
[C]くらきのち[G]からの [C]よしふせ[G]ぐとも
kuraki no chikara no, yoshi fusegu tomo
[Am]主のみこ[F]とばこそ [C]すすみに[G]すす[C]め
shu no mikotoba koso, susumi ni susume
[C]わがいの[F]ちも [C]わが妻子(つまこ)[G]も
waga inochi mo, waga tsumako mo
[Am]とらばと[Em]りね [F]神の国[G]は
toraba torine, kami no kuni wa
[C]なおわ[F]れに[C]あ[G]り[C]
nao ware ni ari
{end_of_verse}
